dev_plans.content: LIGHTWEIGHT HEURISTIC kBET (no external kBET library, single-seed, fixed subsample). Goal: a cheap, deterministic proxy for batch mixing that can run every epoch without blowing the training budget. What is measured: for each cell in a fixed stratified subsample of ~1500 cells, look at its k=0.10*N kNN neighbors in the scVI latent space and compute a chi-squared goodness-of-fit statistic between the local neighbor batch counts and the global batch proportions; the metric is the fraction of tested cells whose p-value >= 0.05 (i.e. null NOT rejected = batch is locally well mixed). Pipeline insertion: hook into the SCVI trainer's `on_epoch_end` callback; once per epoch, temporarily flip `model.is_trained_=True`, move the module to CPU (mid-training the dataloader yields CPU tensors), call `model.get_latent_representation(adata)`, then restore device + flag. Step-by-step: (1) On the first record() call, encode `adata.obs['batch']` to integer codes and draw ONE stratified subsample of 1500 cells (proportional to global batch ratios, seed=42) — cache indices for the rest of training so the metric is comparable across epochs. (2) Each epoch, compute the latent embedding for those 1500 cells only (subindex adata). (3) Build a sklearn `NearestNeighbors(n_neighbors=k+1)` graph with k=max(10, floor(0.10*1500))=150, drop the self column. (4) For a fixed set of 300 test centres (seed=42, no resampling across epochs), compute observed batch counts in each neighborhood, expected = k * global_batch_fraction, stat = sum((observed-expected)^2/expected), p = chi2.sf(stat, dof=n_batches-1). (5) acceptance = 1 - (count of p<0.05)/300. (6) Append (epoch, acceptance) to a live JSON at `.agent_probe/live/probe_live.json` and to an in-memory series. (7) On conclude(), compute tail mean over the last 5 epochs, write `.agent_probe/metric/probe_result_N.json` and a plotly PDF chart with standard/acceptable dashed lines. Output: per-epoch float in [0,1], live time-series JSON, final result JSON with conclusion string, plotly PDF chart. Tradeoff: single-seed + small subsample gives noisy per-epoch values (sigma ~0.03-0.05) but is fast (~10s/epoch on 1500 cells) and dependency-free; the tail-mean averaging in conclude() absorbs the noise.
dev_plans.metric: Mean kBET acceptance rate over the last 5 epochs (fraction of fixed 300 test neighbourhoods in a 1500-cell stratified subsample whose chi-squared goodness-of-fit p-value against global batch proportions is >= 0.05), recorded once per epoch.
dev_plans.standard_threshold: 0.80 — Büttner et al. 2019 report acceptance ~0.8-0.9 for well-integrated atlases (scVI on PBMC cross-batch) and <0.3 for raw log-counts; 0.80 is the upper end of what a correctly configured scVI run reaches on a 2-3 batch atlas.
dev_plans.acceptable_threshold: 0.55 — a single round of naive scVI on a moderately batched atlas typically lands at 0.55-0.65 acceptance; below this the batch effect is still visibly dominant in UMAP and worth a fix-loop iteration.

dev_plans.content: STATISTICALLY RIGOROUS kBET (full Büttner 2019 protocol, multi-seed, per-batch stratified, variance-stabilised). What is measured: the per-batch kBET acceptance rate (one number per batch) AND the overall mean acceptance, with variance across batches and across subsample seeds reported as a confidence band. This is the proper Büttner test, not a proxy. Pipeline insertion: same `on_epoch_end` hook as the lightweight plan, but the computation per epoch is heavier — budget ~30-60s/epoch on a 5k-cell atlas. Step-by-step: (1) Cache THREE stratified subsamples of N=2500 cells each (seeds 42,43,44), each preserving global batch ratios; reuse the same three subsamples every epoch. (2) For each subsample s: build a kNN graph with k=max(25, floor(0.10*N))=250 using sklearn `NearestNeighbors` on the scVI latent of the subsampled cells. (3) For EACH batch b, restrict test centres to cells OF batch b (this is the per-batch variant — Büttner tests whether a cell of batch b sees neighbourhood batch compositions consistent with the global null). Sample up to 400 test centres per batch (seed=42+s). (4) For each centre c, observed = batch counts in c's kNN neighbourhood (excluding c itself), expected = k * global_batch_fractions (computed from the FULL adata, not the subsample, to avoid subsampling bias in the null), stat = sum((observed-expected)^2/expected), dof = n_batches-1, p = scipy.stats.chi2.sf(stat, dof). Reject if p < 0.05. (5) acceptance_b_s = 1 - rejection_fraction_for_batch_b_subsample_s. (6) Per-epoch metric = mean over b and s of acceptance_b_s. ALSO compute per-batch variance = var_b(mean_s acceptance_b_s) and seed-variance = mean_b(var_s acceptance_b_s); both are recorded into the result JSON as auxiliary fields (the plot only shows the mean). (7) NaN-safety: if a batch has <50 cells in a subsample, skip it in that subsample (do not contribute to the mean) rather than divide-by-zero; if all batches are skipped, record NaN for the epoch (the live writer should accept NaN and interpolate). (8) Edge case: if n_batches==1, kBET is undefined — emit 1.0 trivially and warn. (9) On conclude(): tail mean over last 5 epochs drives PASS/FAIL; ALSO require per-batch variance < 0.10 (auxiliary gate, logged but not gating the chart) so a run that mixes 2 of 3 batches well and 1 badly isn't credited. Output: per-epoch mean acceptance float, auxiliary per-batch CSV at `.agent_probe/metric/kbet_per_batch_epoch_N.csv`, live JSON, final JSON with tail_mean/per_batch_variance/seed_variance, plotly PDF with mean line + shaded per-batch-variance band.
dev_plans.metric: Mean kBET acceptance rate across batches and subsample seeds (mean over 3 stratified subsamples of 2500 cells, each contributing a per-batch acceptance rate = 1 - fraction of 400 same-batch test centres whose chi-squared goodness-of-fit p-value against the full-atlas global batch proportions is < 0.05), recorded once per epoch.
dev_plans.standard_threshold: 0.80 — Büttner 2019's Figure 4 / scIB Luecken 2022 report kBET acceptance ~0.80-0.85 for scVI / scANVI / BBKNN on the PBMC 10x multi-batch atlas; 0.80 sits at the upper end of plausible for a 2-4 batch atlas trained end-to-end with default early stopping.
dev_plans.acceptable_threshold: 0.55 — the lower bound of 'good' methods in the scIB benchmark; below 0.55 the method is on par with logcounts-only or worse, indicating the integration term in the ELBO is being dominated by reconstruction.

dev_plans.content: LIBRARY-BASED kBET via `scib_metrics.kBET` (canonical implementation, minimal custom code, future-proof against kBET definition drift). What is measured: the official scib_metrics kBET acceptance score on the scVI latent, computed by the maintained scib-metrics package (which implements the Büttner 2019 test with its own k selection, subsampling, and p-value correction — matching what the scIB leaderboard reports). Pipeline insertion: same `on_epoch_end` hook; scib_metrics works on an `AnnData` with `obsm['X_emb']` set to the scVI latent and `obs['batch']` as the batch key. Step-by-step: (1) On the first record() call, cache a single stratified subsample of 2000 cells (seed=42, proportional to global batch ratios) and cache the corresponding `AnnData` view with `batch` as a categorical column. scib_metrics kBET has its own internal subsampling but pre-subsampling keeps per-epoch cost bounded. (2) Each epoch: get `model.get_latent_representation(adata_sub)` (CPU flip + `is_trained_=True` dance as in the other plans), write it into `adata_sub.obsm['X_emb']`. (3) Call `scib_metrics.kBET(adata=adata_sub, batch_key='batch', embedding='X_emb', n_neighbors=50, n_pcs=None, alpha=0.05, subsample=None)` — the returned object has `.score()` (mean acceptance) and `.per_batch_score()` (dict batch->acceptance). (4) Record mean acceptance as the per-epoch metric. (5) For reproducibility, set `scib_metrics`'s internal RNG via `np.random.seed(42)` before the call (scib_metrics uses the global numpy RNG for its test-centre sampling). (6) NaN-safety: if `.score()` returns NaN (degenerate batch with one cell, etc.), fall back to writing NaN for the epoch and skip; do not crash the training loop. (7) On conclude(): tail-mean over last 5 epochs -> PASS/FAIL; write final JSON with `mean_acceptance`, `per_batch_acceptance` dict (last epoch), and a plotly PDF chart. Dependency note: `pip install scib-metrics>=0.5` (pulls scanpy, anndata, scipy); add to the project's pyproject. Output: per-epoch float, live JSON, final JSON with per-batch dict, plotly PDF.
dev_plans.metric: scib_metrics kBET mean acceptance score (scib_metrics.kBET(...).score()) on a 2000-cell stratified subsample of the scVI latent embedding with batch_key='batch' and alpha=0.05, recorded once per epoch.
dev_plans.standard_threshold: 0.80 — scib-metrics' own reference benchmark (Luecken 2022 scIB reproduction) reports scVI at kBET ~0.78-0.85 on the PBMC atlas; 0.80 matches what the canonical library reports for a correctly-tuned scVI run.
dev_plans.acceptable_threshold: 0.55 — the scIB 'acceptable integration' lower envelope; below 0.55 scib-metrics ranks the method below logcounts+BBKNN and a fix-loop iteration is warranted.
